title: ficus scassellatii subsp. thikaensis
intro: Ficus scassellatii subsp. thikaensis, also known as the Thika Ficus, is a subspecies of the Ficus scassellatii tree native to Kenya. It is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The Thika Ficus has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and providing antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
